tldr.title: Five frontier LLMs disagree on 67% of 1k real-world fact-check claims
tldr.author: kostaj
bullets: Frontier LLMs fail to establish a reliable consensus on factual claims, rendering single-model automated fact-checking highly untrustworthy for production.
Developers must implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and multi-model voting architectures to mitigate hallucination and factual drift in high-stakes applications.
Disagreement rates underscore how proprietary training data mixtures, alignment heuristics, and temporal cutoffs prevent cross-vendor consistency on ambiguous real-world facts.

tldr.title: What are some of your favourite developer tools?
tldr.author: dhruvp
bullets: Modern CLI search and filtering tools like ripgrep (rg) and fzf drastically speed up codebase navigation and command-history retrieval.
Environment managers like direnv and version switchers like asdf automate project-specific configurations, reducing runtime and dependency conflicts.
Neovim paired with LSP engines and tmux remain top choices for highly customizable, distraction-free terminal-based development environments.

tldr.title: Atom Exhaustion Is Not a Footgun. It's One Third of Our CVEs
tldr.author: pushcx
bullets: Dynamic creation of Erlang/Elixir atoms from untrusted input exhausts the VM's non-garbage-collected atom table, causing immediate DoS crashes.
This memory exhaustion vector accounts for nearly one-third of CVEs in BEAM-based systems, indicating systemic architectural risks.
Prevent exploitation by auditing code for unsafe functions like `String.to_atom` and mandating `String.to_existing_atom` for untrusted input.

tldr.title: What's cooking on SourceHut? Q2 2026
tldr.author: kmaasrud
bullets: SourceHutÔÇÖs CI platform (builds.sr.ht) advances its KVM-based build isolation, offering faster ephemeral VM provisioning for secure pipeline runs.
Enhanced hosting infrastructure scales Mercurial (hg.sr.ht) and Git performance, prioritizing raw ssh-based push/pull efficiency over heavy web UI overhead.
Updates to self-hosted SourceHut deployments simplify multi-tenant configurations, database migrations, and mail-based CI dispatch integrations.

tldr.title: Bad cholesterol slashed 62% by single dose of gene-editing drug in small trial
tldr.author: Beth Mole
bullets: Verve Therapeutics' single-dose gene-editing drug VERVE-101 reduced LDL cholesterol by 62% in an interim 35-patient Phase I trial.
The therapy utilizes in vivo base editing to permanently turn off the PCSK9 gene in the liver.
This represents the first clinical proof-of-concept for utilizing CRISPR-based base editing to treat a common cardiovascular disease.
